# Pantry Index

Last updated: 2026-05-13

Scratch storage for fragments, snippets, half-finished paragraphs, quotes,
ideas you can't yet place, and gathered research. Nothing in here gets
compiled into the book. Move material into `chapters/` when ready to use it.

Tic TOC's `/scaffold` command reads this directory when synthesizing the
four planning files (vision.md, architecture.md, chapters-spec.md, risks.md).
Anything you put here becomes input to the planning layer.

---

## Research notes (generated by Chapter Research Gatherer, 2026-05-13)

| File | Chapter | Description |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `01-the-slideument-problem_notes.md` | Ch 1 | Slide vs. document — Reynolds' slideument coinage, Mayer's Redundancy Principle, NASA Columbia / Tufte case |
| `02-no-clear-hierarchy_notes.md` | Ch 2 | Typographic hierarchy, Gestalt figure-ground, Mayer's Signaling Principle, Bringhurst, NN/g eye-tracking |
| `03-too-much-text_notes.md` | Ch 3 | Mayer's Coherence + Modality Principles, text-density research, Kosslyn PowerPoint critique, split-attention |
| `04-the-wrong-visual-form_notes.md` | Ch 4 | Cleveland & McGill perceptual ranking, Munzner viz taxonomy, when bullets fail vs. tables/diagrams/timelines |
| `05-color-is-doing-nothing-or-harm_notes.md` | Ch 5 | ColorBrewer, WCAG 2.1 contrast (4.5:1 AA), color-blind safety, Heer & Stone color naming |
| `06-the-textbook-figure-on-the-slide_notes.md` | Ch 6 | Pre-Training + Segmenting Principles, Ware's perception, OpenStax figure case, AI-image failure mode (1-line scope per OQ5) |
| `07-seductive-details_notes.md` | Ch 7 | Garner/Gillingham/White 1989 (origin), Harp & Mayer 1998, Rey 2012 meta-analysis, Coherence Principle |
| `08-the-deck-that-covers-but-doesnt-teach_notes.md` | Ch 8 | Wiggins & McTighe backward design, retrieval practice (Karpicke & Roediger), Bjork desirable difficulties, Cepeda spacing |
| `09-live-deck-vs-study-artifact_notes.md` | Ch 9 | Mayer Modality Principle, Means et al. 2010 online vs. F2F meta-analysis, Reynolds vs. Atkinson, two-artifact workflow |
| `10-the-headline-that-says-nothing_notes.md` | Ch 10 | Atkinson Beyond Bullet Points, Garner & Alley 2013 + Wolfe et al. 2024 AE structure research, Minto Pyramid |
| `11-owning-your-design-md_notes.md` | Ch 11 | Brad Frost Atomic Design, W3C Design Tokens (stable Oct 2025), SLDS, Lupton Thinking with Type 3rd ed., prompting-as-design |
| `12-the-diagnostic-checklist_notes.md` | Ch 12 | Gawande Checklist Manifesto, Haynes 2009 NEJM (WHO surgical safety), Pronovost 2006 NEJM, Bosk 2009 critique, READ-DO vs. DO-CONFIRM |

## Library files (copied from shared MD library)

Located at `/Users/bear/Documents/CoWork/bear-textbooks/MD`. Large source files
copied as `_lib_*` for inline reference. Not read in full by research agents
— each is a 200KB–2MB transcript; chapter authors should grep into them as needed.

| File | Relevant to | Notes |
|------|------------|-------|
| `_lib_EdTech.md` | Ch 1, 8, 9 | Educational technology compendium — broad pedagogy + tools context |
| `_lib_How-to-Deliver-a-TED-Talk.md` | Ch 1, 9, 10 | Presentation craft — directly relevant for live-deck and headline chapters |
| `_lib_Teaching-for-Deeper-Learning.md` | Ch 8 | Backward-design / meaning-making pedagogy — pairs with Wiggins & McTighe |
| `_lib_The-Digital-Delusion-Horvath.md` | Ch 1, 3, 7, 8 | Cognitive science of learning critique of digital instruction — central for cog-load chapters |
| `_lib_Building-Thinking-Classrooms.md` | Ch 8, 9 | Active-learning pedagogy — relevant for "coverage vs. teaching" framing |
| `_lib_Surfaces-and-Essences.md` | Ch 6, 7 | Hofstadter on analogy — relevant for figure abstraction and seductive-detail mechanism |
| `_lib_Thinking-Fast-and-Slow.md` | Ch 7, 10 | Kahneman on dual-process cognition — relevant for cognitive load and "headline as System 1 answer" framings |

## Other pantry contents (pre-existing)

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `lecture-slides-deep-research.md` | 22KB foundational research on Sweller CLT + Mayer's 15 principles + seductive details. Cross-chapter foundation — every chapter notes file references it. **Do not duplicate; reference from Section F.** |
| `brutalist-vision.md` | The Brutalist system overview — "maximally informed, minimally autonomous" framing. Context for every chapter; central for Ch 11. |
| `brutalist/` (subdir: CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, PROJECT.md) | Example Brutalist system files. Reference for Ch 11 (owning your DESIGN.md). |
| `D3a.md` | Companion Brutalist D3 graphicacy book draft. Relevant for Ch 6 (textbook figures), though TIKTOC.md OQ3 decision states Ch 6 is self-contained. |
| `.gitkeep` | Git directory marker |
